metadata.description: Sample assessment for NovaPay, an L1.5 Series A fintech startup. Early-stage AI adoption with ad-hoc tool usage, no standards, no metrics. Verdict: NEEDS_FOUNDATIONS, Track A.
metadata.samplePurpose: Demonstrates Track A routing for teams in the Emerging band

interviewScores.name: AI Tooling Landscape
keyFindings: Three developers use Claude Code but with personal configurations
No CLAUDE.md or shared AI tool configuration
Two developers still using ChatGPT copy-paste workflow
No Kiro IDE adoption

interviewScores.name: Development Workflow & Specs
keyFindings: No spec-driven development practice in place
AI prompts are ad-hoc, not documented or reused
Feature requirements exist in Jira but are not formatted as AI specs
Team lead expressed strong interest in structured AI workflows

interviewScores.name: CI/CD & Quality
keyFindings: GitHub Actions pipeline exists with basic lint and test steps
No eval gates or AI-specific quality checks
Test coverage at 42%, below industry average
No differentiation between AI-generated and human-written code in CI

interviewScores.name: Metrics & Visibility
keyFindings: No AI-specific metrics tracked at all
No dashboard or executive visibility into AI usage
Cannot distinguish AI-assisted commits from manual ones
Team lead estimates 30-40% of code is AI-assisted but has no data

interviewScores.name: Governance & Security
keyFindings: No formal AI usage policy
No data handling guidelines for AI tools
PII occasionally passed to AI tools without awareness
No approval process for new AI tool adoption

interviewScores.name: Org & Culture
keyFindings: CTO is enthusiastic about AI-assisted development
Most developers are eager to adopt structured AI workflows
One developer is resistant but open to evidence-based persuasion
Small team size makes adoption changes easier

workshopModules.name: CI/CD & Eval Gates
workshopModules.reason: Too early -- no baseline to gate against

workshopModules.name: Metrics & Dashboards
workshopModules.reason: Too early -- need data flowing first

preWork.task: Install Claude Code and configure for AWS Bedrock
Run verify-setup.sh and confirm green output
Identify 2 features the team will build during the workshop

gapRemediation.action: Define quality rubrics for AI-generated code. Implement automated scoring. Establish minimum acceptance thresholds.
Deploy the EventBridge-Timestream metrics pipeline. Enable token tracking, cost attribution, and latency monitoring.
Create an AI usage governance charter. Define approval workflows for agent autonomy levels. Document data handling policies.

milestones.measurable: Scanner re-run, L2.0+ achieved for Track B upgrade

milestones.milestone: Track B started or extended coaching
milestones.measurable: Full workshop scheduled or coaching plan in place

saTouchpoints.agenda: Walk through pre-work checklist, answer setup questions, confirm workshop date
Deliver Modules 00-02, hands-on exercises, first spec-driven build
Review commit trailer adoption rates, troubleshoot blockers
Review first 2-week metrics, adjust approach if needed
Re-run scanner, conduct brief interview, determine Track B readiness
